metadata.title: Sohni Dharti Remittance Account | Meezan Bank

content: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Meezan Dharti Remittance Account Where Your Hard-Earned Remittance Meets Care, Convenience & Compliance Dharti Remittance Account (MSDRA) is a Shariah-compliant account that makes sending and receiving money to your loved ones safe, easy, and worry-free. Whether you are a Non-Resident Pakistani sending funds or a beneficiary in Pakistan, Remittance Account (MSDRA) helps ensure your hard-earned money reaches your loved ones with the care, trust, and peace of mind you deserve. Key Features & Benefits No initial deposit requirement Free Pay Orders Free Issuance of Debit Card* Free Issuance of Cheque Book (for Current Accounts only) Free Mobile Banking – bank anytime, anywhere Free Online Branch Banking SMS Alerts Service as per Schedule of Charges (SOC) Profit paid on a monthly basis, calculated daily (applicable to Savings Accounts only) *Free Debit Cards include PayPak, VISA Silver, and MasterCard Silver Card. The first debit card is issued free of charge. However, Annual/Replacement/Supplementary Card charges will apply as per SOC. WHY CHOOSE MSDRA Designed for Overseas Pakistanis — Tailored exclusively to meet the needs of Pakistanis living abroad. Trusted Shariah-compliant Banking — Backed by Pakistan’s leading Islamic bank. A Secure Way to Support Your Loved Ones — Send and manage funds in Pakistan with complete confidence. How Does Dharti Remittance Account Work? Dharti Remittance Account is available both a Current Account and a Savings Account, based on the Islamic concepts of Qard and Mudarabah, respectively. Download Fatwa Mudarabah Basis By opening a MSDRA Pak Rupee Savings Account, the customer enters into a Mudarabah relationship with Meezan Bank. In this arrangement, the customer is an Investor (Rab-ul-Maal) and the Bank is the Manager (Mudarib) of the deposited funds. The Bank allocates the funds received from the customers to a deposit pool; which is then utilized to provide financing under Islamic modes including (but not limited to) Murabaha, Ijarah, Istisna and Diminishing Musharakah. Qard Basis The MSDRA Pak Rupee Current Account is based on a Qard contract. Under this arrangement, the Bank may use these funds for investment and other purposes. This account allows you the peace of mind of having your money safely deposited with a bank with the additional assurance that the Bank is not investing your money in activities that contravene Shariah principles.
